name: Abyssinian
funFact: One of the oldest known breeds, the Abyssinian loves climbing and will seek out the highest perch in any room.

name: American Shorthair
funFact: Descended from cats brought aboard ships by European settlers to keep rodents away from cargo.

name: Bengal
funFact: Bred from domestic cats and the Asian leopard cat, giving it a wild, spotted 'mini-leopard' coat.

name: Birman
funFact: Legend says sacred temple cats were rewarded with the breed's signature white 'gloved' paws.

name: Bombay
funFact: Bred to resemble a miniature black panther, right down to its gleaming copper eyes.

name: British Shorthair
funFact: Its plush round face is said to have inspired the Cheshire Cat in Alice in Wonderland.

name: Burmese
funFact: Almost every modern Burmese traces its ancestry to a single cat named Wong Mau.

name: Chartreux
funFact: Famous for its woolly blue-grey coat and an almost silent, rarely heard voice.

name: Cornish Rex
funFact: Has only a soft, wavy undercoat, giving it a curly coat that feels like crushed velvet.

name: Devon Rex
funFact: Nicknamed the 'pixie cat' for its oversized ears, big eyes, and elfin face.

name: Egyptian Mau
temperament: Loyal, active, and reserved with strangers
funFact: The only naturally spotted domestic cat breed, and one of the fastest, hitting speeds over 48 km/h.

name: Exotic Shorthair
funFact: Essentially a short-haired Persian, earning it the nickname 'the lazy man's Persian.'

name: Himalayan
funFact: A Persian-Siamese cross that pairs a fluffy coat with the striking color points of a Siamese.

name: Korat
funFact: Considered a good-luck symbol in Thailand, where they are traditionally given as gifts in pairs.

name: Maine Coon
funFact: One of the largest domestic breeds, the 'gentle giant' can stretch over a metre from nose to tail.

name: Manx
funFact: Known for its taillessness, the result of a natural genetic mutation on its island home.

name: Norwegian Forest Cat
funFact: A staple of Norse legend, its water-resistant double coat is built to survive Scandinavian winters.

name: Ocicat
funFact: Looks as wild as an ocelot but is fully domestic, created from Siamese, Abyssinian, and American Shorthair lines.

name: Oriental Shorthair
funFact: Comes in more than 300 color and pattern combinations, the most of any cat breed.

name: Persian
funFact: One of the oldest and most recognizable breeds, prized for its luxurious, flowing coat.

name: Ragdoll
funFact: Named for its habit of going completely limp and relaxed when picked up.

name: Russian Blue
funFact: Its dense, silver-tipped double coat and vivid green eyes give it a shimmering appearance.

name: Savannah
funFact: A hybrid of domestic cats and the African serval, it can leap more than two metres into the air.

name: Scottish Fold
funFact: Its signature folded ears come from a natural mutation affecting cartilage throughout the body.

name: Siamese
funFact: One of the most famous breeds, known for its deep blue eyes and a loud, conversational 'voice.'

name: Siberian
funFact: Often better tolerated by allergy sufferers thanks to naturally lower levels of the Fel d 1 protein.

name: Sphynx
funFact: Despite appearing bald, its warm skin needs regular bathing to remove built-up body oils.

name: Tonkinese
funFact: A Siamese-Burmese cross that blends the chatty personality and rich coloring of both parents.

name: Turkish Angora
funFact: One of the oldest natural breeds, it often sports striking odd-colored eyes.

name: Turkish Van
funFact: Nicknamed the 'swimming cat' for its unusual love of water and strong swimming ability.
